|  |  | | --- | --- | | Competitive | * [Cross-country skiing](/wiki/Cross-country_skiing_(sport) "Cross-country skiing (sport)")   + [Paralympic](/wiki/Paralympic_cross-country_skiing "Paralympic cross-country skiing") * [Ski jumping](/wiki/Ski_jumping "Ski jumping")   + [Ski flying](/wiki/Ski_flying "Ski flying") * [Nordic combined](/wiki/Nordic_combined "Nordic combined") | | Endurance | * [Ski marathon](/wiki/Ski_marathon "Ski marathon") * [Ski

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ski+mountaineering%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is a skiing discipline that involves climbing mountains either on skis or carrying them, depending on the steepness of the ascent, and then descending on skis. There are two major categories of equipment used: free-heel [telemark skis](/wiki/Telemark_ski "Telemark ski") and skis based on [alpine skis](/wiki/Alpine_ski "Alpine ski"), where the heel is free for ascents, but is fixed during descent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Ski mountaineering is an activity that variously combines [ski touring](/wiki/Ski_touring "Ski touring"), [telemark](/wiki/Telemark_skiing "Telemark skiing"), [backcountry skiing](/wiki/Backcountry_skiing "Backcountry skiing"), and [mountaineering](/wiki/Mountaineering "Mountaineering").[[2]](#cite_note-Volken-2)
Havell, Kim (March 6, 2002). ["Find the Right Gear—Tip from Walter Laserer"](http://www.outsideonline.com/1908946/find-right-gear). Outside Magazine. Retrieved 2016-01-30.

Volken, Martin; Schell, Scott; Wheeler, Margaret (2007). [*Backcountry Skiing: Skills for Ski Touring and Ski Mountaineering*](https://books.google.com/books?id=fbYMocIZ18YC&q=ski+mountaineering&pg=PA338). Outdoor Expert Series. The Mountaineers Books. p. 339. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-59485-038-7](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-59485-038-7 "Special:BookSources/978-1-59485-038-7").

Mather, Victor (2022-02-19). ["What's new for the next Winter Olympics? Ski mountaineering"](https://www.nytimes.com/2022/02/18/sports/olympics/skimo-ski-mountaineering.html). *The New York Times*. [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [0362-4331](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/0362-4331). Retrieved 2025-02-03.
["Work Boots Width Chart"](https://theworkboots.us/boot-width-guide). Theworkboots. 21 August 2024. Retrieved 17 October 2024.

Lind, David A.; Sanders, Scott (2013). [*The Physics of Skiing: Skiing at the Triple Point*](https://books.google.com/books?id=OpjuBwAAQBAJ&q=climbing+skins&pg=PA161) (2 ed.). Springer Science & Business Media. p. 270. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1475743456](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1475743456 "Special:BookSources/978-1475743456"). Retrieved 2026-02-14.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Ski mountaineer](/w/index.php?title=Ski_mountaineer&redirect=no "Ski mountaineer"))

Skiing discipline

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Ski_mountaineering "Special:EditPage/Ski mountaineering") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Ski mountaineering"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ski+mountaineering%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ski+mountaineering%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Examples of ski mountaineering

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/Ski_Mountaineering_Silverton_Colorado.jpg/250px-Ski_Mountaineering_Silverton_Colorado.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ski_Mountaineering_Silverton_Colorado.jpg)

Backcountry, [Colorado](/wiki/Silverton,_Colorado "Silverton, Colorado"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Norway_skiing.jpg/250px-Norway_skiing.jpg)](/wiki/File:Norway_skiing.jpg)

Mountain, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/Steve_Marolt_Skiing_Northeast_ridge_of_everest_in_2007.jpg/250px-Steve_Marolt_Skiing_Northeast_ridge_of_everest_in_2007.jpg)](/wiki/File:Steve_Marolt_Skiing_Northeast_ridge_of_everest_in_2007.jpg)

[High-altitude](/wiki/List_of_ski_descents_of_eight-thousanders "List of ski descents of eight-thousanders"), [Everest](/wiki/Mount_Everest "Mount Everest"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/Adventurer_Patrick_Sweeney.jpg/250px-Adventurer_Patrick_Sweeney.jpg)](/wiki/File:Adventurer_Patrick_Sweeney.jpg)

[Couloir](/wiki/Couloir "Couloir"), [Chamonix](/wiki/Chamonix "Chamonix"), [France](/wiki/France "France")

**Ski mountaineering** is a skiing discipline that involves climbing mountains either on skis or carrying them, depending on the steepness of the ascent, and then descending on skis. There are two major categories of equipment used: free-heel [telemark skis](/wiki/Telemark_ski "Telemark ski") and skis based on [alpine skis](/wiki/Alpine_ski "Alpine ski"), where the heel is free for ascents, but is fixed during descent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Ski mountaineering is an activity that variously combines [ski touring](/wiki/Ski_touring "Ski touring"), [telemark](/wiki/Telemark_skiing "Telemark skiing"), [backcountry skiing](/wiki/Backcountry_skiing "Backcountry skiing"), and [mountaineering](/wiki/Mountaineering "Mountaineering").[[2]](#cite_note-Volken-2)

In 2026, [ski mountaineering racing](/w/index.php?title=Ski_mountaineering_racing&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ski mountaineering racing (page does not exist)")—a distinct sub-discipline—[made its debut](/wiki/Ski_mountaineering_at_the_2026_Winter_Olympics "Ski mountaineering at the 2026 Winter Olympics") in the [2026 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2026_Winter_Olympics "2026 Winter Olympics").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Equipment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ski_mountaineering&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Equipment")]

* [Bindings](/wiki/Ski_binding "Ski binding"): Should be reliable, light, and durable.[[4]](#cite_note-USSMAFAQ-4)
* [Boots](/wiki/Ski_boot "Ski boot"): Should be light and flexible.[[4]](#cite_note-USSMAFAQ-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Skis](/wiki/Ski "Ski"): Width: 60–90 mm to balance performance and weight. Skinnier skis lack performance in softer snow but are lighter while wider skis lack performance in firmer snow and are heavier. Lightweight skis make ascent easier. Length depends on a person's height but going shorter than your normal length will be lighter and will be easier on steep terrain.
* [Ski skins](/wiki/Ski_skins "Ski skins"): Attached to bottoms of skis, used for walking up slopes.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* [Rope](/wiki/Rope "Rope"): Are not always used in ski mountaineering, but if the approach to the summit or drop-in is treacherous, having a rope can help mitigate risk. When choosing a rope, one needs to make sure it is a proper climbing rope, paracord and ropes found in gas stations (or any rope not intended specifically for mountain climbing), are not suitable. Some popular climbing rope companies are Sterling, Mammut and Black Diamond.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* [Crampons](/wiki/Crampons "Crampons"): Are used to help walk and climb on hard packed snow and ice. They usually have 10 or 12 spikes and are secured to your boot using three different methods. The first method is strap-on crampons which are attached by stepping into a basket and then securing with webbing and these fit with any type of boot. The second is hybrid or semi-automatic crampons. These use the welt on the back of your boot along with a basket in the front to hold onto your boot, and also use webbing to help secure them; these are made to work with a certain type of boot. The final type of crampon is an automatic or step-in crampon. These use both the back and front welt on a person's boot with webbing to secure them to the boot. These types of crampons are the most secure, but also the most specialized crampon and need specific boots to have them work.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* Touring bindings: Lightweight alpine touring or telemark bindings. For AT, the pin style toes are the lightest and tour the best.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* [Avalanche search and rescue equipment](/wiki/Avalanche_rescue#Search_and_rescue_equipment "Avalanche rescue"): [Beacon](/wiki/Avalanche_transceiver "Avalanche transceiver") (transceiver) – digital transceiver, Probe – at least 2 meters in length, Shovel – any snow shovel works but collapsible shovels are more lightweight and packable, Snow kit- used to help examine snow and snow quality.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* [Ice axe](/wiki/Ice_axe "Ice axe"): Used to assist safety in steep snow and ice. No longer than 60 cm, 50–55 cm is preferred.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
